query_normalized: exploring the validity of the weak equivalence principle with publicly available planetary orbital data physics
rationale: The paper investigates the Weak Equivalence Principle using ultracold neutrons and quantum effects, which is a distinct experimental domain (subatomic particles in a lab) from the user's specific question regarding "publicly available planetary orbital data" (macroscopic celestial mechanics). While the core physical principle is the same, the paper fails the "off-domain entirely" rejection rule because it does not measure the specific variables (planetary orbits) or utilize the specific data typ
